Class BaseCharacterProfile

java.lang.Object
logic.character.BaseCharacterProfile
All Implemented Interfaces:
CharacterProfile
Direct Known Subclasses:
ArcaneScholar, GirlBlueScholar, GirlGreenScholar, GirlPurpleScholar, GirlRedScholar, GreenScholar, PurpleScholar, RedScholar
public abstract class BaseCharacterProfile extends Object implements CharacterProfile
Shared implementation of CharacterProfile that derives all resource paths from the character's gender and element.

Concrete subclasses only need to call the protected constructor with their specific stats; all getter implementations are inherited from here.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructor
    Description
    protected
    BaseCharacterProfile(String id, String displayName, String gender, String element, int maxHp, int focusGainPerCorrectAnswer)
    Initialises all fields, auto-deriving image paths from gender and element.

    Method
    Description

    displayName()
    Display name shown in the character selection screen.
    String
    element()
    Element key that determines spell damage, slow, and visual effect ("red", "blue", "green", or "purple").
    int
    focusGainPerCorrectAnswer()
    Amount of focus gained each time the player answers a question correctly.
    String
    gender()
    Gender key used for resource path construction ("boy" or "girl").

    int

    Maximum hit points for this character.
    String
    npcResource()
    Top-down NPC sprite path — used for the student tile on the game board.
    String
    portraitResource()
    Walk sprite sheet path — shown in character select and battle screens.
    String
    spellResource()
    Spell-cast sprite sheet path — shown in character select element cards.
    String
    walkResource()
    Walk animation sprite sheet path.

  • Constructor Details

    • BaseCharacterProfile

      protected BaseCharacterProfile(String id, String displayName, String gender, String element, int maxHp, int focusGainPerCorrectAnswer)
      Initialises all fields, auto-deriving image paths from gender and element.
      Parameters:
      id - unique character identifier (e.g. "boy-blue")
      displayName - name shown in the character selection UI
      gender - "boy" or "girl"
      element - "red", "blue", "green", or "purple"
      maxHp - maximum hit points
      focusGainPerCorrectAnswer - focus gained per correct quiz answer
